title: She’s running Boston for patients ‘rebuilding their lives step by step’
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Lina Sinisterra Mazariegos, 44, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
Originally from Cali, Colombia, Mazariegos views running as a way to understand herself and process emotions.
She supports Spaulding patients' recovery, symbolizing perseverance and shared hope in their rehabilitation journeys.

title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed energetically at the House of Blues in Boston, showcasing both new and classic hits.
Frontman Howlin’ Pelle Almqvist engaged the audience with vibrant stage presence and charismatic commentary.
Australian band The Chats opened the concert with an energetic, punk-infused set as a prelude to The Hives.

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, age 42 from Braintree, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the New England Patriots Foundation.
In 2023, McHugh was diagnosed with Stage 3 breast cancer but continued to pursue her passion for running.
Despite chemotherapy, surgeries, and joint pain, McHugh returned to running with support from her late mentor, Susan Hurley.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey and others urged Meta to address privacy risks of facial recognition in its smart glasses.
Lawmakers warned the technology could identify and surveil people at protests, potentially chilling free expression.
Meta was asked to explain data collection and protection measures by April 6; future plans for the glasses are unclear.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council delayed a vote on $2.65 million funding for PFAS-free firefighting gear.
Current firefighter gear contains PFAS, chemicals linked to cancer, prompting calls for new protective equipment.
Councilors seek more time to review costs and durability; firefighters urge immediate approval for safety.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Mayor Cathleen DeSimone proposes converting Attleboro's Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve and wildlife rehabilitation center.
The zoo needs $57 million in improvements; it currently costs $900,000 annually from Attleboro's general fund to operate.
Capron Park Zoo officials express concern over the plan, prioritizing animal care and stability during the transition.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Watertown couple Abigail Brown and Colin Wong have a last-minute microwedding due to blizzard disruptions in January 2026.
FX's "Love Story" series evokes nostalgia for 1990s communication without digital distractions, according to columnist Kara Baskin.
Discussion arises on the evolving use of 'partner' in relationships, questioning its interchangeability with 'boyfriend'.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback of Burlington is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH to honor his father.
Averback, a testicular cancer survivor, began running after the loss of his father who was treated at MGH.
Despite health setbacks, Averback views the marathon as a tribute to endurance and personal resilience.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots strong interest in WR A.J. Brown persists; Eagles rejected recent trade offer.
Philadelphia may wait until June 1 to trade Brown for salary cap reasons.
Brown has been working out with new Patriots safety Kevin Byard, fueling trade speculation.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens recorded 47 points in 34 games, ending his sophomore season at Boston College.
The Boston Bruins anticipate Hagens signing an entry-level contract to join the team for the season's final stretch.
Hagens, the seventh pick in the 2025 NHL Draft, is seen as a future top-six player for the Bruins.
